title: Leaves turning inside out
observation: Trees showing the silvery underside of their leaves.
effect: An approaching storm or high winds are likely.

title: Low-flying swallows
observation: Swallows and other birds flying very close to the ground.
effect: Insects are driven down by atmospheric pressure, signaling rain.

title: Red sky at night
observation: The sunset turns the western horizon a deep, vibrant red.
effect: Clear, stable weather is moving in from the west.

title: Cows lying down
observation: An entire herd of cattle decides to lie down in the pasture.
effect: A change in weather, typically precipitation, is imminent.

title: Increased fragrance of flowers
observation: Gardens and woods smell much stronger than usual.
effect: High humidity is intensifying the scent, often preceding rain.
